items.headline: Albemarle: A Different Storage Boom
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation is regaining momentum as lithium prices rebound, driven by surging energy storage systems (ESS). ALB Q1 2026: EPS 2.95, EBITDA 664M adjusted. ESS demand +117% YTD. Trading ~6x adjusted EBITDA. Strong balance sheet with significant cash flow upside if lithium

items.headline: Albemarle Wins Analyst Upgrades As Lithium Expansion Story Gains Support
items.snippet: Citi and RBC upgrades center on brownfield expansions at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs. Analyst support suggests execution on projects and cost discipline will be key.

items.headline: Energy storage demand grows 40% annually
items.snippet: In the next two years, distribution of lithium demand between EVs and energy storage will be more balanced. ALB CCO Eric Norris: Grid energy storage is more evenly distributed globally, a very interesting demand driver. ESS production globally growing 35-40% annually.

items.headline: Does Albemarle Premium Valuation Justify Buying Now
items.snippet: ALB expects lithium demand CAGR 10-20% 2025-2030. Stationary storage significant driver alongside EVs. ESS production +117% YoY Q1 2026. Operating CF 346M, FCF 248M in Q1. Liquidity 2.7B including 1.1B cash.

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: RBC Capital Markets: Albemarle likely to continue growing volumes from brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, Kings Mountain and Antofalla. 2026 and 2027 EBITDA targets 2.8B and 2.9B respectively. Lithium supply expected to remain tight through 2027. Price target raised to
